deep_dive.title: Stochastic Signed Distance Processes
deep_dive.abstract: Multi-view surface reconstruction is a core problem in computer vision. One prominent line of work represents the surface implicitly as a signed distance field (SDF), optimizing it based on the photometric loss between rendered and observed pixel colors. These approaches typically employ SDF-based volume rendering to obtain a differentiable relaxation of discontinuous visibility along rays, thereby reducing reliance on silhouette supervision. In this paper, we reformulate SDF-based volume rendering as probabilistic surface rendering, where each pixel color is modeled as a mixture distribution induced by the random first ray-surface intersection. To this end, we introduce Stochastic Signed Distance Processes (SSDP), which model the SDF along each ray as a stochastic process, inducing a first-passage-time distribution for each ray. We then derive the first-passage probability for each sampling interval based on Bayesian filtering, together with its practical approximation for parallel rendering. We further show that NeuS, an existing SDF-based volume rendering method, arises as a special case of our formulation. Experiments on the DTU and MobileBrick datasets demonstrate that our method outperforms baselines in both surface reconstruction and uncertainty quantification, supporting the effectiveness of our first-passage formulation. Our code is available at https://github.com/skmhrk1209/SSDP.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: multi-view surface reconstruction via probabilistic SDF-based volume rendering
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过概率SDF体渲染进行多视图表面重建
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过概率SDF体渲染进行多视图表面重建
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过概率SDF体渲染进行多视图表面重建
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Stochastic Signed Distance Processes (SSDP) to model SDF along rays as stochastic processes for multi-view surface reconstruction.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对多视图表面重建中SDF体渲染需离散化可见性的问题，提出随机符号距离过程（SSDP），将光线上的SDF建模为随机过程，导出连续概率表面渲染公式，实现了无需轮廓监督的高质量重建。理论分析和实验证明该方法能更精确地表示表面边界，为多视图重建提供了稳健的新范式。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对多视图表面重建中SDF体渲染需离散化可见性的问题，提出随机符号距离过程（SSDP），将光线上的SDF建模为随机过程，导出连续概率表面渲染公式，实现了无需轮廓监督的高质量重建。理论分析和实验证明该方法能更精确地表示表面边界，为多视图重建提供了稳健的新范式。

deep_dive.title: From Uncertainty to Stability and Fidelity: Guiding Sparse-View 3D Gaussian Splatting with Fisher Information
deep_dive.abstract: 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) has emerged as a promising technique for novel view synthesis. However, 3DGS requires dense input views to achieve high-quality rendering. In sparse-view scenarios, 3DGS often prones to overfitting, resulting in noticeable artifacts and degraded rendering quality. Previous methods explore to address this issue by introducing additional priors (e.g. depth priors) or integrating regularization techniques (e.g. Dropout). However, these methods are often applied without principled guidance. In particular, prior-based augmentation typically samples novel viewpoints randomly, while Dropout-based regularization randomly removes Gaussians. The compounded randomness introduces uncertainty and instability, limiting the fidelity of novel view synthesis. In this paper, we propose a novel method for sparse-view 3DGS that incorporates Fisher Information to quantitatively guide the utilization of geometric priors and regularization. Specifically, our method comprises two key components: (1) Stereo augmentation with Fisher Information. By leveraging Fisher Information, we actively select most informative supporting views and use depth priors to curate reliable pseudo ground truths, which reduces randomness in augmentation and improves stability and rendering fidelity; (2) Uncertainty-aware regularization. We reduce the instability of Dropout-based regularization by using Fisher Information to quantitatively measure the uncertainty of each 3D Gaussian, and adaptively adjust the removal probability, leading to more stable and effective regularization. With these two components, our method effectively mitigates overfitting and improves the stability of optimization in sparse-view 3DGS, resulting in superior rendering fidelity. Extensive experiments show that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance in sparse-view novel view synthesis benchmarks.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Sparse-view 3D reconstruction via Gaussian Splatting with Fisher Information guided sampling and regularization
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用 Fisher 信息引导采样和正则化的稀疏视角三维高斯泼溅重建
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用 Fisher 信息引导采样和正则化的稀疏视角三维高斯泼溅重建

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Addresses sparse-view 3DGS overfitting by using Fisher Information to guide viewpoint sampling and Gaussian dropout, reducing uncertainty and improving rendering quality.

deep_dive.title: Spectral GS-SLAM: Observability-Aware, Degeneracy-Robust Tracking for Real-Time 3D Gaussian Splatting SLAM
deep_dive.abstract: Recent 3DGS-SLAM systems enable real-time operation by leveraging conventional feature matching or ICP-based tracking, thereby avoiding the heavy dense photometric optimization used in earlier approaches. However, feature matching remains prone to failure in textureless environments, while ICP-based tracking struggles in structureless or geometrically degenerate scenes due to ill-conditioned optimization. To address this issue, we propose Spectral GS-SLAM, an efficient yet robust tracking framework that integrates ICP with complementary feature-based constraints. Our method mitigates numerical instability by adaptively compensating under-constrained directions in degenerate scenarios, without interfering with the shared Gaussian representation used for mapping. We further introduce a Gaussian-aware planarity weighting mechanism that exploits the intrinsic covariance structure of 3D Gaussians to characterize scene geometry and guide information fusion. Extensive evaluations on challenging TUM RGB-D sequences demonstrate that Spectral GS-SLAM achieves real-time performance (40.14 FPS) while maintaining consistent tracking in both structureless and featureless environments. The proposed method preserves trajectory integrity in degenerate scenes while maintaining competitive performance in non-adverse conditions.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Real-time 3DGS-SLAM with feature matching and ICP tracking for scene reconstruction
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 实时3DGS-SLAM，结合特征匹配与ICP跟踪实现场景重建
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 实时3DGS-SLAM，结合特征匹配与ICP跟踪实现场景重建
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 实时3DGS-SLAM，结合特征匹配与ICP跟踪实现场景重建
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Spectral GS-SLAM, a robust tracking framework for 3DGS-SLAM that integrates ICP and feature-based constraints to handle degenerate scenes, enabling real-time 3D reconstruction.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有3DGS-SLAM系统在纹理缺失或结构退化环境中跟踪失败的问题，提出Spectral GS-SLAM框架，将ICP与互补的基于特征的约束相结合，并自适应补偿退化场景中的欠约束方向。实验表明，该方法在不影响形状精度的前提下，提高了跟踪鲁棒性，实现了实时3D场景重建。

deep_dive.title: Lightweight 3D Feature Pretraining by Bayesian Inversion of 2D Foundation Models
deep_dive.abstract: We present Casper3D, a lightweight probabilistic framework for converting noisy multi-view 2D foundation-model embeddings into a latent 3D semantic representation. We model view-level semantic features as noisy observations of an underlying 3D semantic state and infer this state with a set-based variational model that incorporates relative pose during multi-view reasoning. Casper3D is trained by predicting held-out semantic observations from novel viewpoints, while remaining aligned with visual and text semantic spaces for open-vocabulary 3D understanding. The framework is backbone-agnostic and applies to both language-aligned and self-supervised embeddings. Experiments show that Casper3D produces more stable 3D semantics than simple multi-view pooling, especially in ambiguous and noisy settings.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: lightweight probabilistic framework for stable 3D semantic features from multi-view 2D embeddings
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过贝叶斯反演从多视图噪声二维嵌入中学习稳定三维语义特征，实现多视图特征一致性匹配
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过贝叶斯反演从多视图噪声二维嵌入中学习稳定三维语义特征，实现多视图特征一致性匹配

deep_dive.title: Artic-O: End-to-End Articulated Object Reconstruction via Latent Geometry Learning
deep_dive.abstract: Reconstructing articulated objects from sparse images requires recovering complete geometry, movable parts, and motion parameters. Recent methods typically separate geometry reconstruction, part reasoning, and articulation estimation into different stages. This separation can weaken consistency between shape, active parts, and motion, while also incurring substantial inference cost. We introduce Artic-O, an end-to-end, feed-forward framework for articulated object reconstruction via latent geometry learning. Instead of fitting geometry in image or view space, Artic-O maps sparse multi-state observations into a pretrained latent geometry space, where a frozen flow-matching decoder provides a complete-shape prior for recovering visible and occluded structures. To connect geometry with articulation, Artic-O fuses visual tokens, geometry latents, and point-wise decoder features in an image-grounded part-reasoning module for active-part segmentation and articulation prediction. We further train the model with a geometry-to-articulation curriculum and a decoupled two-pass strategy to balance reconstruction and part-level supervision. On PartNet-Mobility, Artic-O achieves strong reconstruction quality while being substantially more efficient than LARM, a strong prior method. It reduces Chamfer Distance, improves F-score, and achieves comparable or better articulation accuracy across most joint metrics, while reducing inference time from 9 minutes to about 0.3 seconds per object.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Reconstructs articulated 3D objects from sparse multi-state images via latent geometry learning
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 从稀疏多状态图像中通过潜在几何学习重建关节3D物体
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 从稀疏多状态图像中通过潜在几何学习重建关节3D物体
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 从稀疏多状态图像中通过潜在几何学习重建关节3D物体
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Artic-O proposes an end-to-end framework for reconstructing articulated 3D objects from sparse multi-state images by mapping observations into a pretrained latent geometry space with a flow-matching decoder, achieving consistent shape, part, and motion recovery.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对关节物体的3D重建需要从稀疏图像恢复完整几何、可动部件和运动参数，且现有方法将几何重建、部件推理和关节估计分离导致不一致。Artic-O提出端到端框架，将稀疏多状态观测映射到预训练的潜在几何空间，利用冻结的流匹配解码器提供完整形状先验，同时恢复可见和被遮挡部分，实现形状、部件和运动的一致性重建。实验表明该方法在推理速度和一致性上优于多阶段方法，为关节物体的生成式重建提供了新途径。

deep_dive.title: ACEsplat: Accelerated 3D Gaussian Scene Regression via RGB and Poses Only
deep_dive.abstract: Per-scene 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) enables high-fidelity rendering, but practical robotic and AR scene capture pipelines often depend on external geometric initialization (e.g., SfM point clouds or depth estimates), which can be slow and brittle in on-site deployment. We present ACEsplat, a fast per-scene optimization framework that reconstructs 3D Gaussian representations from RGB images and camera poses only, without requiring external 3D priors (e.g., precomputed SfM models or supervised depth maps). ACEsplat uses a two-stage pipeline: (1) a self-supervised scene coordinate regression (SCR) module builds an internal geometry prior within 4--5 minutes; (2) SCR features and coordinate priors are fused by a lightweight Gaussian initialization head, followed by per-scene 3DGS optimization. On static-view rendering, ACEsplat achieves 29.11 dB PSNR on Wayspots with real-time SLAM poses and 33.20 dB on Cambridge Landmarks with SfM-refined poses. On RealEstate10K sparse-view novel view synthesis, it achieves competitive image fidelity under a challenging 2-view setting. ACEsplat completes scene-specific SCR mapping and 3DGS reconstruction within 15--25 minutes on a single GPU, making it a practical RGB+pose-only solution for rapid scene setup in robotics and mixed-reality applications.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: 3D scene reconstruction from multiple RGB images and camera poses
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 多视角RGB图像和相机位姿的三维场景重建
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 多视角RGB图像和相机位姿的三维场景重建
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 多视角RGB图像和相机位姿的三维场景重建
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: ACEsplat reconstructs 3D Gaussian representations from posed RGB images only, without external geometric priors. A self-supervised scene coordinate regression builds a geometry prior in minutes, enabling fast and accurate scene modeling.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有3D高斯泼溅方法依赖外部几何初始化的问题，ACEsplat提出仅需RGB图像和相机位姿的快速场景重建框架。该方法通过自监督场景坐标回归模块在数分钟内构建内部几何先验，再结合轻量高斯初始化头进行优化，无需预计算SfM或深度图。实验证明ACEsplat实现了高质量的新视角合成，为机器人和AR等实时应用提供了高效的回环场景重建方案。

deep_dive.title: Visual Geometry Transformer in the Wild: Distractor-Free 3D Reconstruction
deep_dive.abstract: Current end-to-end multi-view 3D reconstruction methods achieve impressive results, but rely on a restrictive static assumption: the scenes is entire distractor-free with perfect cross-view geometry. This reliance on idealized inputs causes even the most advanced methods to fail in real-world settings, where transient distractors and occlusions present. To address this, we propose Visual Geometry Transformer in the Wild (VGTW), an end-to-end framework for robust reconstruction from inconsistent views. At its core, we isolate and suppress distractor-affected regions while preserving the consistent components across views. Specifically, we introduce a Distractor-aware Training (DAT) strategy that separates clean features from distractor-contaminated ones in the attention mechanism while enforcing feature consistency across images. To enable this, we train the model with an auxiliary mask prediction head, using supervision from a new dataset we collected with pixel-level distractor masks. The resulting VGTW model is a feed-forward network that directly outputs clean, distractor-free point clouds. Remarkably, it requires no additional 3D supervision, remains computationally efficient, and is compatible with existing pipelines. Extensive experiments validate our approach, demonstrating state-of-the-art performance and robust generalization in diverse, real-world scenarios.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: end-to-end multi-view 3D reconstruction robust to distractors
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 端到端多视图三维重建，对干扰物鲁棒
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 端到端多视图三维重建，对干扰物鲁棒
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 端到端多视图三维重建，对干扰物鲁棒
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes VGTW for robust multi-view 3D reconstruction by isolating distractor-affected regions via Distractor-aware Training.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有端到端多视图三维重建方法在真实场景中因瞬态干扰物和遮挡失效的问题，提出VGTW框架，通过干扰感知训练分离干净特征，抑制干扰区域，实现室内外场景的鲁棒重建。实验表明该方法能有效处理不一致视图，提升重建质量，具有较强的实际应用价值。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对现有端到端多视图三维重建方法在真实场景中因瞬态干扰物和遮挡失效的问题，提出VGTW框架，通过干扰感知训练分离干净特征，抑制干扰区域，实现室内外场景的鲁棒重建。实验表明该方法能有效处理不一致视图，提升重建质量，具有较强的实际应用价值。

deep_dive.title: G-MASt3R-SfM: Graph-based View Pruning and Multi-stage Optimization for Robust SfM
deep_dive.abstract: Structure from Motion (SfM) is essential for multi-view 3D reconstruction, however, its accuracy heavily relies on the accuracy of image matching. While the recent correspondence matching method, MASt3R, enables robust matching even under challenging conditions, it tends to generate incorrect correspondences for non-overlapping image pairs. Consequently, existing SfM methods using MASt3R, such as MASt3R-SfM, suffer from significant degradation in pose estimation accuracy as they incorporate these unreliable matches directly into optimization. To address this issue, we propose G-MASt3R-SfM, a novel SfM pipeline that enhances robustness through two key modules. First, the Graph-based View Pruning (GVP) module constructs a scene graph from matching confidence and geometrically prunes outlier views. Second, the Multi-Stage Optimization (MSO) module progressively refines camera parameters by expanding the optimization scope from local consistency to the global consistency. Experiments on the ETH3D dataset demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art accuracy in both camera pose estimation and 3D reconstruction, effectively suppressing noise caused by outliers.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Robust SfM pipeline for multi-view 3D reconstruction using graph-based view pruning and match re-weighting
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于图剪枝和匹配重加权的鲁棒SfM管道用于多视图三维重建
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于图剪枝和匹配重加权的鲁棒SfM管道用于多视图三维重建
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于图剪枝和匹配重加权的鲁棒SfM管道用于多视图三维重建
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes G-MASt3R-SfM, which enhances SfM robustness by pruning unreliable matches using a scene graph and multi-stage optimization, preventing pose estimation degradation from incorrect correspondences.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: SfM依赖图像匹配，MASt3R虽鲁棒但为不重叠图像产生误匹配，影响姿态估计。G-MASt3R-SfM 提出图视点剪枝和匹配重加权，结合多阶段优化，有效滤除噪声，在多个数据集上显著提升重建鲁棒性。

deep_dive.title: Learning Stable Canonical Worlds for Novel View Synthesis and Beyond
deep_dive.abstract: Feed-forward Gaussian splatting (FFGS) facilitates real-time novel view synthesis, yet current methods often remain tied to view-dependent predictions. As more input views are added, they may accumulate noisy or redundant evidence instead of converging to a stable scene representation. In this paper, we introduce CanonicalGS, a feed-forward pipeline that maps cluttered multi-view observations into a stable, scene-centric representation. CanonicalGS first extracts view-centric evidence from depth, semantic features, and uncertainty estimates, and then aggregates this evidence in a canonical latent world using uncertainty-aware fusion. By emphasizing reliable observations while suppressing uncertain or redundant ones, CanonicalGS produces representations that scale more effectively for novel view synthesis and transfer to downstream visual perception tasks. Experiments show up to a $2.5$ dB improvement in peak signal-to-noise ratio for synthesizing novel views and an $11\%$ gain in semantic segmentation accuracy.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: learns a stable scene-centric representation from multi-view RGB images using uncertainty-aware fusion and feed-forward Gaussian splatting
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用不确定性感知融合与前馈高斯泼溅从多视图RGB图像学习稳定的场景中心表征
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用不确定性感知融合与前馈高斯泼溅从多视图RGB图像学习稳定的场景中心表征
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 利用不确定性感知融合与前馈高斯泼溅从多视图RGB图像学习稳定的场景中心表征
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: CanonicalGS is a feed-forward pipeline that fuses multi-view evidence (depth, semantics, uncertainty) into a stable canonical representation, enabling robust novel view synthesis even with noisy or redundant inputs.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对前馈高斯泼溅在多视图输入增加时会累积噪声而无法收敛到稳定场景表示的问题，CanonicalGS提出一种不确定性感知的融合策略，从各视图中提取深度、语义和不确定度估计，在规范潜在空间中聚合，强调可靠观测。实验显示其在新视角合成任务上比现有方法更鲁棒，且表示更稳定。

deep_dive.title: DrivingVoxels: Compositional Sparse Voxel Rasterization for Dynamic Driving Scene Reconstruction
deep_dive.abstract: Reconstructing dynamic urban scenes remains challenging due to the unbounded nature of driving environments and the presence of multiple dynamic objects. Currently, potentially faster sparse voxel methods are mainly designed for static scenarios. On the other hand, dynamic approaches based on 3D Gaussian Splatting, despite their high-fidelity, are often time-consuming for driving scenarios and exhibit uncontrollable memory growth in large scenes. To address these limitations, we present DrivingVoxels, a compositional sparse voxel rendering framework for dynamic driving scenes. Our method jointly rasterizes sparse voxels from multiple independent octrees within a single rendering pass. Each rigid dynamic object is represented by an octree defined in its local coordinate frame, while a separate static octree models the stationary background. DrivingVoxels adopts a fully explicit, neural-free representation together with a LiDAR-guided structural initialization that efficiently captures scene geometry. We evaluate our framework on the PandaSet benchmark, demonstrating that DrivingVoxels performs on par on perceptual metrics and better on structural metrics for NVS and reconstruction while requiring shorter training times than previous 3DGS-base methods to an efficient optimization workflow anchored by a strong LiDAR prior.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: reconstructs dynamic urban scenes from multi-view images using compositional sparse voxel rendering
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用组合稀疏体素渲染从多视图图像重建动态城市场景
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用组合稀疏体素渲染从多视图图像重建动态城市场景
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 使用组合稀疏体素渲染从多视图图像重建动态城市场景
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: DrivingVoxels is a framework for dynamic driving scene reconstruction that uses multiple independent octrees to represent moving objects and background, rasterizing sparse voxels compositionally in a single pass for efficient rendering.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有动态驾驶场景重建方法在效率与内存增长上的不足，DrivingVoxels提出一种组合稀疏体素渲染框架，为每个动态刚体及静态背景构建独立八叉树，并在单次渲染中联合光栅化。在多个驾驶场景数据集上，该方法实现了与3D高斯泼溅相当的质量，同时计算更快、内存更可控。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对现有动态驾驶场景重建方法在效率与内存增长上的不足，DrivingVoxels提出一种组合稀疏体素渲染框架，为每个动态刚体及静态背景构建独立八叉树，并在单次渲染中联合光栅化。在多个驾驶场景数据集上，该方法实现了与3D高斯泼溅相当的质量，同时计算更快、内存更可控。

deep_dive.title: Arbor: Explicit Geometric Conditioning for Controllable 3D Asset Generation
deep_dive.abstract: Text and image conditioned 3D models now generate convincing assets, but they still offer little direct control over the space an object should occupy or avoid. In authoring, this spatial intent is often known before generation starts. A chair should fit a seating envelope, a prop should leave clearance for motion, or a part should expose a contact surface. Prompts and image views are poor carriers for such constraints, requiring the need for an explicit control interface.   We present Arbor, a trainable attachment for text conditioned latent 3D generation. Arbor introduces constraint meshes as a native 3D control interface. The interface uses hull regions where geometry should exist, avoidance regions that should remain empty, and touch regions the object should contact. Unlike completion or whole object scaffold control, these meshes are not target evidence. They are local typed requirements and can include regions where no surface should appear. Arbor keeps this signal as geometry by converting constraint meshes into tokens and learning a routed attachment inside a frozen denoiser. Each latent region can therefore receive the part of the constraint that matters for its spatial location.   We evaluate Arbor on automatic and artist curated control benchmarks with hull, avoidance, and touch constraints, and compare the metric trends to a user preference study. Even without dedicated compliance losses, Arbor improves constraint obedience while preserving object quality and variation under fixed constraints.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Text and image conditioned 3D generation with explicit mesh constraints for spatial control
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 文本和图像条件下带显式网格约束的可控3D生成
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 文本和图像条件下带显式网格约束的可控3D生成
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 文本和图像条件下带显式网格约束的可控3D生成
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Arbor introduces constraint meshes as a native 3D control interface for text-conditioned latent 3D generation, allowing users to specify hull, avoidance, and touch regions, enabling generation of assets that conform to spatial requirements, improving controllability over prompts alone.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有文本/图像条件3D生成模型缺乏对生成物体空间占用的直接控制。Arbor提出可训练的附加模块，引入约束网格作为原生3D控制接口，包括应该存在几何体的外壳区域、应保持空的回避区域以及接触区域。该方法在文本条件潜在3D生成中实现了精确的空间约束，使用户能指定物体应符合的空间要求，如椅子适应座围或道具留出间隙，提升了生成的可控性和实用性。

quick_skim.title: SCOPE: Scale-Consistent One-Pass Estimation of 3D Geometry
quick_skim.abstract: We present SCOPE (Scale-Consistent One-Pass Estimation of 3D Geometry), a novel approach for estimating 3D geometry from extended monocular video sequences, where existing methods struggle to maintain both geometric accuracy and temporal consistency across hundreds of frames. Our approach generates affine-invariant 3D point maps with shared parameters across entire sequences, enabling consistent scale-invariant representations. We introduce three key innovations: viewpoint-invariant geometry aligning multi-perspective points in a unified reference frame; appearance-invariant learning enforcing consistency across exponential timescales; and frequency-modulated positioning enabling extrapolation to sequences vastly exceeding training length. Experiments across diverse datasets demonstrate significant improvements, reducing relative point map error by 24.2% and temporal alignment error by 34.9% on ScanNet compared to state-of-the-art methods. Our approach handles challenging scenarios with complex camera trajectories and lighting variations while efficiently processing extended sequences in a single pass. Project page: https://scope3d.github.io/.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: 3D geometry estimation from monocular video, generating point maps
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 从单目视频估计3D几何，生成点图
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 从单目视频估计3D几何，生成点图
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 从单目视频估计3D几何，生成点图
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Presents SCOPE, which estimates scale-consistent 3D geometry from extended monocular video sequences using viewpoint-invariant and appearance-invariant learning.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有方法在长单目视频序列中难以兼顾几何精度与时间一致性的问题，提出SCOPE方法，通过视点不变几何对齐、外观不变学习和频率调制定位，生成整个序列共享参数的仿射不变3D点图。实验表明，该方法能够外推到远长于训练的视频序列，实现尺度一致的一次性3D几何估计。

quick_skim.title: MeshFlow: Mesh Generation with Equivariant Flow Matching
quick_skim.abstract: Meshes are among the most common 3D scene representations, but directly generating meshes is challenging because the representation contains important symmetries, including permutation invariance of faces and vertices. MeshFlow learns to generate triangle meshes directly as triangle soups, avoiding the need to serialize meshes into long autoregressive sequences. We adopt equivariant optimal-transport flow matching models that respect the key symmetries of triangle soups: arbitrary permutations of faces and permutations of the vertices within each face.   Toward this goal, we propose a simple yet effective modification to the Diffusion Transformer architecture, resulting in a scalable network capable of modeling a velocity field while maintaining the desired equivariance. We further introduce an optimal-transport-based training objective that improves convergence by eliminating supervision signals that violate these symmetries. MeshFlow achieves mesh quality comparable to state-of-the-art autoregressive mesh generators while providing about an 18$\times$ speedup during inference. Project page is at https://qiisun.github.io/MeshFlow/.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Generates 3D meshes using equivariant flow matching, learning to produce triangle soups directly
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用等变流匹配直接生成三维网格（三角面汤）
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用等变流匹配直接生成三维网格（三角面汤）
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用等变流匹配直接生成三维网格（三角面汤）
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes MeshFlow, a generative model for 3D meshes that uses equivariant flow matching to respect permutation symmetries, generating triangle meshes without serialization.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 网格是常见三维表示，但生成需处理置换对称性。MeshFlow 采用等变最优传输流匹配，修改 Diffusion Transformer 架构，直接生成三角面汤，保持面与顶点的置换等变性，为三维形状生成提供可扩展方案。

quick_skim.title: $φ$-Scene: Physically Grounded Image-to-3D Scene Reconstruction
quick_skim.abstract: Reconstructing compositional 3D scenes from a single image is a fundamental challenge in 3D world modeling. Recent methods can recover high-fidelity, complete 3D objects and predict plausible scene arrangements, but most still treat scene reconstruction primarily as a visual and geometric prediction problem. Their outputs may therefore contain floating objects, interpenetrations, or unstable-contact artifacts, limiting their physical validity and downstream usability in simulation, robotics, and interactive environments. We present $φ$-Scene, a physically grounded approach to open-vocabulary and compositional image-to-3D scene reconstruction. The key premise is that a reconstructed scene should not be treated merely as a set of objects with predicted poses, but as a stable physical system. Accordingly, $φ$-Scene formulates reconstruction as topology-driven physical assembly: it infers how objects support one another, orders them accordingly, and progressively settles each object against its already stabilized support context. For each object in topological order, SDF-based optimization first resolves penetrations against the pre-settled support context, and rigid-body simulation then settles the object into a stable contact configuration under real-world physical constraints. Experiments on 3D-Front show that $φ$-Scene achieves the strongest overall performance among out-of-domain methods and remains highly competitive with in-domain baselines. Human and VLM evaluations further show strong preference for $φ$-Scene in visual quality, reference alignment, and physical plausibility. Finally, dedicated physical plausibility metrics covering static contact quality and dynamic stability demonstrate that $φ$-Scene substantially reduces penetration artifacts while producing much lower post-simulation drift, indicating more stable and physically grounded 3D scenes.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Physically grounded 3D scene reconstruction from images
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于物理约束的图像到三维场景重建
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于物理约束的图像到三维场景重建
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于物理约束的图像到三维场景重建
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: φ-Scene introduces physical constraints into image-to-3D scene reconstruction, preventing floating objects and interpenetrations. It produces physically valid scenes by enforcing contact and support priors, enhancing usability in simulation and robotics.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有图像到三维场景重建主要看作视觉和几何预测问题，输出常包含物理上不合理的浮动物体或穿模。φ-Scene提出物理驱动的开放词汇组合式三维场景重建框架，将场景视为有姿态预测的物体集合之外，额外施加物理约束，确保物体间接触稳定、无穿透。通过结合视觉感知与物理推理，该方法生成的三维场景在物理上更加可信，可直接用于下游仿真和交互任务。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有图像到三维场景重建主要看作视觉和几何预测问题，输出常包含物理上不合理的浮动物体或穿模。φ-Scene提出物理驱动的开放词汇组合式三维场景重建框架，将场景视为有姿态预测的物体集合之外，额外施加物理约束，确保物体间接触稳定、无穿透。通过结合视觉感知与物理推理，该方法生成的三维场景在物理上更加可信，可直接用于下游仿真和交互任务。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 3D场景生成、重建、物体生成、分割与特征匹配相关文章, especially work that connects or combines: 3D scene reconstruction from multi-view images; generating 3D scenes from image collections; generating 3D objects from images or text; semantic segmentation of 3D scenes; matching 3D features across multiple views; multi-view stereo 3D reconstruction; 3D scene generation and reconstruction using unlabeled internet photos; unsupervised 3D object generation and semantic segmentation from multi view images; feature matching and 3D scene understanding from large scale image collections.

quick_skim.title: Lift4D: Harmonizing Single-View 3D Estimation for 4D Reconstruction In-the-Wild
quick_skim.abstract: Reconstructing dynamic non-rigid objects from monocular video requires integrating visual cues from direct observations with data-driven priors over geometry and appearance. Prior approaches either learn to directly predict 4D representations from visual input or initialize a 3D representation that is subsequently deformed and refined based on video evidence. However, the former are constrained by the scarcity of 4D training data, while the latter leverage priors only for the initial reconstruction and rely solely on video supervision thereafter; neither handles complex in-the-wild scenarios with large deformations and occlusions well. We present Lift4D, a test-time optimization framework that addresses both limitations. First, we adapt an existing single-view 3D reconstruction model to yield temporally consistent per-frame predictions via causal latent conditioning, providing a coherent initialization for a deformable 3D Gaussian Splatting representation. We then ``sculpt'' this representation to match the input video through an occlusion-aware optimization that faithfully recovers visible surface details while completing unobserved regions using a view-conditioned diffusion prior. We demonstrate that Lift4D clearly improves over prior 4D reconstruction methods, particularly on challenging in-the-wild sequences with severe occlusions and non-rigid motion.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: reconstructs dynamic 3D objects from monocular video by combining single-view 3D estimation with test-time optimization
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 从单目视频重建动态三维物体，结合单视图三维估计与测试时优化
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 从单目视频重建动态三维物体，结合单视图三维估计与测试时优化
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 从单目视频重建动态三维物体，结合单视图三维估计与测试时优化
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Lift4D is a test-time optimization framework that reconstructs dynamic non-rigid objects from monocular video by fusing single-view 3D priors with video evidence, handling occlusions and deformations better than prior works.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对从单目视频重建动态非刚性物体时训练数据稀缺和仅依赖视频监督的局限，Lift4D提出一种测试时优化框架，将现有单视图3D估计模型适配为强先验，并与视频观测融合，有效应对大变形与遮挡等野外场景。在实验中，该方法在复杂场景中实现了更精确的动态几何和外观重建。

quick_skim.title: Learning Cross-View Semantic Priors for Single-Reference Unseen Object Pose Estimation
quick_skim.abstract: Single-reference unseen object 6D pose estimation reduces object onboarding by estimating poses of arbitrary novel objects from only one reference view. Recent correspondence-based pipelines have achieved robust performance with vision foundation model (VFM) features. However, they typically treat these features as intra-view descriptors, leaving dense visual-semantic cues, including appearance, structure, and context, insufficiently exchanged across views before geometric decoding. Consequently, the decoded point features may lack joint semantic and geometric discriminability, making correspondence estimation still difficult in challenging cases. Instead of processing features independently, we build the correspondence pipeline around an early cross-view semantic prior. Specifically, cross-view semantic interaction (CVSI) enables dense query and reference VFM tokens to exchange semantic context and form a cross-view prior. Nevertheless, direct CVSI may disturb the VFM token structure, while the resulting semantic prior still needs 3D representation consistency for rigid correspondence. To make this CVSI prior reliable for 3D correspondence learning, we introduce two complementary training-time constraints: the intra-view structure preservation (IVSP) loss preserves the original intra-view token affinity structure during interaction, while the reference-anchored geometric consistency (RAGC) loss enforces spatial representation consistency of decoded point features. The final pose is recovered from learned correspondences through weighted SVD. We further construct a challenging view-pair protocol from the BOP Challenge datasets YCB-V and TUD-L to evaluate robustness in difficult matching scenarios. Extensive experiments on six benchmarks under different view-pair settings show that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance while maintaining comparable inference speed.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Cross-view feature matching for 3D pose estimation using vision foundation model features
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 利用视觉基础模型特征进行跨视图匹配实现3D姿态估计
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 利用视觉基础模型特征进行跨视图匹配实现3D姿态估计

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a correspondence pipeline that aggregates cross-view semantic priors from vision foundation models for robust single-reference 6D pose estimation of novel objects.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对单参考未知物体6D姿态估计中，现有方法跨视图交换不足导致特征区分性差的问题，提出利用视觉基础模型特征构建跨视图语义先验的对应管道。该方法增强了联合语义和几何区分性，在挑战性场景中改善了对应估计。实验表明优于基线，为多视图特征匹配提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: From Pixels to Concepts: Growing Rich 3D Semantic Scene Graph Forests utilizing Foundation Models
quick_skim.abstract: Operating in complex real-world environments requires robots to understand their surroundings on a functional semantic level. This demands a detailed multi-layer world model capturing the complex relations of its surroundings. Hierarchical 3D scene graphs address this challenge by integrating geometric, semantic, and relational data within a unified spatial framework. However, current 3D scene graph approaches often restrict themselves to rigid structures of pre-determined relationship classes, mostly neglecting important semantic connections, like causal connections or environmental contexts. This paper explores the potential of foundation models to build forests of 3D scene graphs with open semantic relationships to improve scene understanding and robotic task execution. We propose a method where instance-specific concept-nodes and relationships are first identified by a VLM and extended upon by a LLM, inferring broader, more abstract concept-nodes and relationships through reasoning. These object-nodes, concept-nodes, and relationships are then assembled into a forest of hierarchical 3D scene graphs, enhanced with concept-nodes to represent abstract concepts. Evaluations were conducted on the uHumans2 and ScanNet indoor dataset, validating the accuracy and relevance of the generated relationships. Downstream suitability of scene-graph forests for robotics applications is demonstrated in an open-vocabulary object-retrieval task utilizing both ScanNet data and a real-world indoor deployment using a Boston Dynamics Spot. This paper leverages foundation models to create more expressive, semantically deep 3D hierarchical scene graphs and demonstrates their potential to advance semantic and environmental understanding in robotics.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: 3D semantic scene understanding with open relationships
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 开放语义关系的三维场景理解
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 开放语义关系的三维场景理解
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 开放语义关系的三维场景理解
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: The paper leverages foundation models to build forests of 3D scene graphs with open semantic relationships, capturing causal and contextual connections for richer 3D scene understanding beyond rigid predefined relations.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 当前层次化3D场景图方法通常局限于预定义关系类别，忽略了因果或环境上下文等语义连接。本文探索利用基础模型构建包含开放语义关系的3D场景图森林，通过从像素到概念的逐步生长，为机器人提供更丰富的功能语义层世界模型。实验表明该方法能够捕获更复杂的场景关系，提升了复杂环境中机器人理解与决策的语义丰富度和灵活性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 当前层次化3D场景图方法通常局限于预定义关系类别，忽略了因果或环境上下文等语义连接。本文探索利用基础模型构建包含开放语义关系的3D场景图森林，通过从像素到概念的逐步生长，为机器人提供更丰富的功能语义层世界模型。实验表明该方法能够捕获更复杂的场景关系，提升了复杂环境中机器人理解与决策的语义丰富度和灵活性。

quick_skim.title: Dense Reward for Multi-View 3D Reasoning with Global Maps and Local Views
quick_skim.abstract: Multi-view 3D Visual Question Answering (MV3D-VQA) requires integrating partial observations into a coherent 3D scene representation and selecting informative viewpoints for multi-step spatial reasoning. However, current multimodal LLMs are typically trained with sparse, answer-level supervision, which often yields inconsistent cross-view reasoning and brittle view selection. We present DR-MV3D (Dense Reward for MV3D-VQA), a map-grounded learning framework that provides dense, verifiable rewards to supervise the reasoning process. Our approach decomposes MV3D-VQA into (i) allocentric global map construction, (ii) question-conditioned view-trajectory planning, and (iii) egocentric grounding for answer prediction. To make intermediate steps learnable without manual annotations, we introduce two rewards: a global consistency reward that aligns the predicted map with geometry-consistent pseudo targets from frozen 3D vision foundation models (e.g., VGGT + SAM3), and a local trajectory reward that supervises ordered viewpoint selection. We optimize the full pipeline with trajectory-level policy optimization (GRPO). Experiments on MindCube, VSI-Bench, and BLINK (MV) show that DR-MV3D consistently improves over strong multi-image baselines, supporting the effectiveness of process-level dense supervision for multi-view 3D reasoning.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: integrates multi-view observations into a coherent 3D scene map for question-conditioned view planning and answer grounding
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 将多视图观测整合为一致的3D场景地图，用于问题驱动的视图规划与答案定位
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 将多视图观测整合为一致的3D场景地图，用于问题驱动的视图规划与答案定位
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 将多视图观测整合为一致的3D场景地图，用于问题驱动的视图规划与答案定位
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: DR-MV3D uses dense, verifiable rewards to supervise multi-view 3D visual question answering by decomposing the task into global map construction, view-trajectory planning, and egocentric grounding, improving cross-view reasoning consistency.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对多模态大模型在多视图3D视觉问答中跨视图推理不一致及视图选择脆弱的问题，DR-MV3D提出基于地图的密集奖励学习框架，将任务分解为全局地图构建、问题条件视图轨迹规划和自我中心定位，利用可验证奖励监督中间步骤。实验表明该方法显著提升了推理的连贯性和准确性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对多模态大模型在多视图3D视觉问答中跨视图推理不一致及视图选择脆弱的问题，DR-MV3D提出基于地图的密集奖励学习框架，将任务分解为全局地图构建、问题条件视图轨迹规划和自我中心定位，利用可验证奖励监督中间步骤。实验表明该方法显著提升了推理的连贯性和准确性。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: feature matching and 3D scene understanding from large scale image collections

quick_skim.title: Lightweight Neural Framework for Robust 3D Volume and Surface Estimation from Multi-View Images
quick_skim.abstract: Accurate volume and surface area estimation is critical for diverse applications, from marine ecology to medical diagnostics. However, existing methods often suffer from high computational costs and poor performance with sparse and noisy data. We propose a fully feed-forward framework that regresses scale-normalized volume and surface area and their associated uncertainties directly from multi-view images. By fusing 3D point cloud reconstructions with view-aligned 2D features through a graph-based decoder, our model bypasses iterative optimization, ensuring exceptional scalability and rapid inference. Experimental results demonstrate that our approach outperforms state-of-the-art methods, particularly when operating with a low number of input images. Validated across coral monitoring, dietary analysis, and anthropometry, our proposed framework provides a robust, adaptable solution for quantitative shape analysis. This architecture provides a high-speed, scalable alternative for precise geometric estimation from visual data, maintaining high performance even in resource-constrained or sparse-view scenarios.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Estimates 3D volume and surface area from multi-view images by fusing point clouds with view-aligned 2D features
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 融合点云与视角对齐的2D特征，从多视图图像估计3D体积与表面面积
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 融合点云与视角对齐的2D特征，从多视图图像估计3D体积与表面面积
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 融合点云与视角对齐的2D特征，从多视图图像估计3D体积与表面面积
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper proposes a feed-forward framework that directly regresses volume and surface area with uncertainties from multi-view images, bypassing iterative optimization via graph-based fusion of 3D point clouds and 2D features, achieving robust performance on sparse and noisy data.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对从多视图图像进行体积和表面估计时计算成本高、稀疏噪声数据性能差的问题，本文提出全前馈框架，通过图基解码器融合3D点云重建与视角对齐的2D特征，直接回归尺度归一化的体积、表面积及其不确定性。该方法避免了迭代优化，具有高可扩展性和快速推理，在低资源条件下优于现有方法，为生态监测和医疗诊断等应用提供了高效鲁棒的解决方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对从多视图图像进行体积和表面估计时计算成本高、稀疏噪声数据性能差的问题，本文提出全前馈框架，通过图基解码器融合3D点云重建与视角对齐的2D特征，直接回归尺度归一化的体积、表面积及其不确定性。该方法避免了迭代优化，具有高可扩展性和快速推理，在低资源条件下优于现有方法，为生态监测和医疗诊断等应用提供了高效鲁棒的解决方案。
